title: Normalize ActionData.Tool to canonical form; remove dual-checks
description:
## Problem
`ActionData.Tool` has a wire-name inconsistency:
- `BlueprintExecutor` sets `Tool = "PlaceBlock"` (C# class name)
- `PlaceBlockTool.ExecuteAsync` sets `Tool = ActionProtocol.Place` = `"place"` (wire protocol name)
- `AgentBackgroundService` has dual-checks at line 1547: `action.Tool == "PlaceBlock" || action.Tool == "place"`

This latent schism means any new code that writes `ActionData` directly could use the wrong form.

## Desired Outcome
`ActionData.Tool` normalized to canonical wire protocol name at construction. Dual-checks removed.

## Scope
- Add normalization in `ActionData` constructor or factory method
- Update `BlueprintExecutor` to use `ActionProtocol.Place`
- Remove dual-checks in `AgentBackgroundService`
- Update any other call sites

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `ActionData.Tool` always contains wire protocol name (lowercase)
- [ ] `BlueprintExecutor` uses canonical constant
- [ ] No `"PlaceBlock"` string literal remains in `AgentBackgroundService`
- [ ] All existing tests pass (including build goal tests)
- [ ] No new build warnings
